title: Rattle Variant BOM
description: Output of the rattle-bom-architect agent / rattle-bom-builder skill. Describes a configurable variant BOM — parts, part placements (Part↔Area edges) and bom_items (Parent↔Child edges) with the full usage_subclauses DSL and option_scalings descriptors — ready to be applied via rattle-config-builder (ensure_part / ensure_part_placement / ensure_bom_item). Field names and types match the backend create-side Pydantic schemas (PartCreateRequest, PartPlacementCreateRequest, BomItemCreateRequest — all extra="forbid"), except that edges reference parts by part_number instead of part_id; the builder resolves numbers to ids. The normative pre-flight validator is skills/rattle-bom-builder/scripts/validate_variant_bom.py — where this schema and that script disagree on STRUCTURE, the script wins. Two of its rules are NOT expressible in JSON Schema and are enforced there only: (1) parent_part_number != child_part_number on every bom_item; (2) every member of an (parent, alt_group) pair carries a unique priority. This schema is deliberately stricter than the script in exactly two places, both to catch a payload the runtime would reject or silently ignore: (a) scrap_percent is bounded 0..100 (the script only warns, but BomItemCreateRequest enforces ge=0/le=100 and the API returns 422); (b) groupSelections / areaStatuses / option_scalings keys must match ^[0-9]+$ (the script's isinstance-str check can never fire on JSON, but a name-keyed entry silently never matches at runtime).

tenant.description: Tenant slug the BOM belongs to (matches the RATTLE_API_KEY_<TENANT> convention).

product_id.description: Live product id the BOM is authored against. Placements must sit on areas assigned to this product.

parts.description: Part master records. Natural key is part_number (unique within the company).

placements.description: PartPlacement rows — the Part↔Area edge. A part is invisible to the product until it has a placement on an area assigned to that product.

bom_items.description: BomItem rows — the Parent-Part↔Child-Part edge. Multi-level BOMs are pure traversals of these; the same fields apply at every depth. There is no `level` field: level is computed by the explosion engine at runtime, never authored.

validation.description: Result of scripts/validate_variant_bom.py, carried alongside the payload so a reviewer sees what the author already checked.

notes.description: Soft warnings the author wants to bubble up (placeholder part numbers, absolute-vs-additive scaling intent, assumed units).

id_key.description: Object keyed by a STRINGIFIED entity id ("42", never 42). JSON object keys are always strings, so this pattern is what actually catches a name-keyed authoring mistake — usage-subclauses.md § Common pitfalls.

part.description: Maps to PartCreateRequest (extra="forbid"). part_img is NOT accepted here — upload it via the dedicated image route. A part carries no usage_subclauses; only its edges do.

part_number.description: Canonical identifier, unique within the company. Every placement and bom_item references parts by this value.

part_cost.description: INTEGER currency unit per company config — not a decimal and not a decimal string (Field(ge=0)). Ghost parts are forced to 0 server-side; their cost rolls up from children at explosion time.

bom_structure.description: "ghost" = phantom assembly: depth-transparent at explosion time, its children bubble up into the parent's level. Keep ghost nesting ≤ 3 levels — the engine warns beyond that.

custom_fields.description: Tenant-defined extensions. ≤ 16 KB serialised, ≤ 100 keys, key pattern [a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_]{0,49}.

placement.description: Maps to PartPlacementCreateRequest (extra="forbid"). Natural key is (part_id, area_id). Says: "in THIS area, this part is included at quantity Q, subject to usage_subclauses and option_scalings."

part_number.description: The placed part. The builder resolves it to part_id.

area_id.description: The area the part is placed on. Must be an area assigned to the product — a placement on an unassigned area is invisible.

quantity.description: Base quantity. MUST be > 0: Pydantic enforces gt=0 and the API returns 422 on 0 — use 1 even when a range-mode option_scaling will override it at explosion time.

uom.description: Quantity unit. Match it to the number_unit of any numbered option that scales this line.

bom_item.description: Maps to BomItemCreateRequest (extra="forbid"). Natural key is (parent_part_id, child_part_id, alt_group, effective_from, effective_to). Says: "the parent part is built from N × child part, subject to subclauses, scaling, scrap, alternates, and a date window." part_group_id is read-only on the wire — do NOT include it (422). NOTE: parent_part_number != child_part_number is enforced by validate_variant_bom.py, not by this schema.

parent_part_number.description: The parent assembly. Resolved to parent_part_id.

child_part_number.description: The child component. Resolved to child_part_id. Must differ from parent_part_number (no self-reference).

quantity.description: Base quantity per parent. MUST be > 0 (Pydantic gt=0 → 422 on 0). For a range-mode line whose base should not contribute, still author 1 and state the absolute-vs-additive intent in `note`.

scrap_percent.description: Scrap / waste allowance. Applied after option scaling: amount × (1 + scrap/100). Negative is clamped server-side; > 100 logs a warning. Prefer scrap_percent over baking waste into a scaling ratio — scrap shows up separately on cost reports.

alt_group.description: Alternates key. Rows under the same parent sharing an alt_group are filtered by usage_subclauses, sorted by priority (lowest first) then order_index, and only the FIRST survivor is emitted. Free-form but stable (e.g. "spindle").

priority.description: Selection order within alt_group — lowest wins. Every member of an (parent, alt_group) pair MUST have a unique priority; duplicates make the choice order-dependent and validate_variant_bom.py errors on them.

effective_from.description: Start of the date validity window. Must be ≤ effective_to.

effective_to.description: End of the date validity window.

order_index.description: Display order under the parent; the tie-break after priority inside an alt_group.

ghost_part.description: Phantom-assembly flag on the edge. The child's own children bubble up into this level instead of incrementing depth.

note.description: Free-form note. Use it to record the absolute-vs-additive intent of a range descriptor and any unit conversion baked into a ratio.

usage_subclauses.description: Conditional-inclusion DSL, evaluated by app/utils/conditions.evaluate_subclauses. EMPTY LIST = always include (the standard / unconditional line) — the legacy isStandard flag is dropped on save. A non-empty list is folded LEFT-TO-RIGHT with each clause's own operator, with no implicit grouping; the first clause's operator is overridden to the default (OR) on save and cannot change the truth value. Full grammar + 9 worked examples: skills/rattle-bom-builder/references/usage-subclauses.md.

clause.description: One clause. Satisfied iff its area part AND its groupSelections part both pass. A clause carrying none of groupSelections / areaStatuses / areaSubclauses is meaningless: it is DROPPED during normalisation. This schema still accepts it — validate_variant_bom.py classifies it as a WARNING, not an error, and the server takes it without complaint — but never author one.

operator.description: How this clause folds with the running result of all prior clauses. Informational on the first clause (silently overridden to OR); load-bearing from the second clause onward.

groupSelections.description: Map of stringified group_id → list of INTEGER option ids. Satisfied when, for EVERY group listed, at least one of its listed options is selected (any-of within a group, AND across groups).

areaStatuses.description: Map of stringified area_id → required enabled state. Satisfied when EVERY listed area matches (true = must be enabled, false = must be disabled). Keys AND-combine; use areaSubclauses to OR them.

areaSubclauses.description: Nested area-only clauses with their own AND/OR operators. The ONLY way to OR area conditions inside a single clause.

area_clause.description: An area-only clause nested under areaSubclauses.

operator.description: Folds against the running result of the prior area clauses.

areaStatuses.description: Map of stringified area_id → required enabled state.

option_scalings.description: Quantity scaling, keyed by the STRINGIFIED id of a NUMBERED option (is_numbered: true). A scaling against a boolean option is a silent no-op — it carries no amount. Independent of usage_subclauses: the subclauses decide WHETHER the line is active, the scalings decide HOW MUCH. Multiple entries resolve independently and their contributions sum. Area-scoped amounts are looked up as "<opt_id>:<area_id>" first, then "<opt_id>". Full reference + 12 worked examples: skills/rattle-bom-builder/references/option-scalings.md.

scaling_descriptor.description: One of the three descriptor shapes the engine recognises.

oneOf.description: LEGACY bare numeric. Ambiguous across code paths (multiplier on the explosion path, per-unit add on the additive snapshot path) — validate_variant_bom.py warns on it. Do not author new ones; use ratio or range.

RATIO — "every `opt` selected → emit `part`". Contribution = selected_qty × part / opt. Both resolution modes give the same number. opt ≤ 0 is a no-op; part = 0 contributes nothing; a missing field defaults to 1.

opt.description: Selected units per emitted part. MUST be > 0 — 0 divides by zero and is treated as a no-op.

part.description: Parts emitted per `opt` selected units. Also carries any unit conversion (e.g. opt 100 / part 1 turns a cm input into m of part).

oneOf.description: RANGE — bracketed lookup. The FIRST bracket whose min ≤ selected ≤ max wins and its `part` is ABSOLUTE (not multiplied by the line's quantity) on the explosion path; on the additive snapshot path it is added to the base. Record which you mean in the line's `note`. No matching bracket → 0 parts.

areas.description: Brackets, ascending and non-overlapping — the first match wins, so order matters. validate_variant_bom.py warns on overlap or descending order.

range_bracket.description: One bracket of a range descriptor. Bounds are expressed in the numbered option's number_unit and are INCLUSIVE.

max.description: Upper bound, inclusive. Omitted / null = +∞. Must be ≥ min.

part.description: The ABSOLUTE part quantity for this bracket. Negative is allowed but the line total is clamped to 0.
